documents.title: Welcome | Dank Linux
documents.description: Welcome to Dank Linux
documents.summary: This document introduces Dank Linux, an integrated desktop environment designed to eliminate the complexity of manually assembling a desktop from numerous disparate tools. It details how the system provides a cohesive, working experience with minimal setup effort.

documents.title: Getting Started | Dank Linux
documents.description: Get started with Dank Linux installation and configuration
documents.summary: This guide provides instructions on how to install and configure the Dank Linux suite across various distributions. It details methods for setting up repositories and offers commands for generating starter configurations or deploying individual settings.

documents.title: DankLinux Repository | Dank Linux
documents.description: Pre-built packages for Fedora, CentOS, Debian, Ubuntu, and OpenSUSE
documents.summary: This document serves as an overview and installation guide for the DankLinux Repository, detailing how to install core desktop components like DankMaterialShell and Niri across various Linux distributions including Debian, Ubuntu, OpenSUSE, Fedora, and CentOS.

documents.title: Support | Dank Linux
documents.description: Get help and support for Dank Linux
documents.summary: This document advises users regarding the proper channel usage for discussions related to DankMaterialShell on the niri discord server and encourages community support for Dank Linux development.

documents.title: Getting Started | Dank Linux
documents.description: Get started with Dank Linux installation and configuration
documents.summary: This guide introduces new users to Dank Linux by explaining how to get started with installing and configuring the entire suite on their operating system.

documents.title: DankLinux Repository | Dank Linux
documents.description: Pre-built packages for Fedora, CentOS, Debian, Ubuntu, and OpenSUSE
documents.summary: This document serves as an overview and comprehensive guide detailing how to install various components of the DankLinux ecosystem, including Desktop Environments like DankMaterialShell and compositors such as Niri, across multiple Linux distributions.

documents.title: Support | Dank Linux
documents.description: Get help and support for Dank Linux
documents.summary: This document serves as a notice regarding where to keep discussions related to the DankMaterialShell on the niri discord server, and it also includes a request for user support for the development of Dank Linux.

documents.description: Get started with Dank Linux installation and configuration
documents.summary: This guide provides instructions on how to get started with installing and configuring the Dank Linux suite across various distributions.

documents.title: DankLinux Repository | Dank Linux
documents.description: Pre-built packages for Fedora, CentOS, Debian, Ubuntu, and OpenSUSE
documents.summary: This document serves as an overview and installation guide for the DankLinux Repository, detailing how to install desktop shells like DankMaterialShell (DMS) and compositors such as Niri across various Linux distributions including Debian, Ubuntu, OpenSUSE, Fedora, and CentOS.

documents.title: Support | Dank Linux
documents.description: Get help and support for Dank Linux
documents.summary: This document serves as a notice reminding users to keep discussions related to `DankMaterialShell` in the appropriate `#dms-*` channels on the niri discord server, and it requests voluntary tips to support Dank Linux development.

documents.title: Overview & Architecture | Dank Linux
documents.description: Modern Wayland desktop shell
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive overview of DankMaterialShell (dms), detailing its features, underlying client-server architecture, component roles, and methods for integrating with the desktop environment such as opening URLs and setting default file associations.

documents.title: Installation | Dank Linux
documents.description: Install DankMaterialShell on your system
documents.summary: This guide details the comprehensive installation process for DankMaterialShell, providing specific instructions tailored for various Linux distributions including Arch, Fedora/CentOS, Debian/Ubuntu, and OpenSUSE.

documents.title: Compositor Setup | Dank Linux
documents.description: <Hero
documents.summary: This document provides detailed configuration instructions for integrating DankMaterialShell (DMS) into various Wayland compositors, specifically focusing on niri and Hyprland. It shows how to include necessary configuration files, set up keybindings, define window rules, and manage startup parameters for seamless integration.

documents.title: Managing Your Installation | Dank Linux
documents.description: Update, configure, and manage DankMaterialShell
documents.summary: This guide provides comprehensive instructions on managing the DankMaterialShell service, covering setup via systemd or manual methods, configuring environment variables for various desktop environments, updating the software across multiple package managers, and details on complete uninstallation and troubleshooting.

documents.title: Overview | Dank Linux
documents.description: Create and install plugins for DankMaterialShell
documents.summary: This document details the structure and types of plugins available for DankMaterialShell, explaining where to install them and outlining different functionalities like widgets, launchers, daemons, and desktop components. It also provides guides on installation, development, and troubleshooting.

documents.title: Layer Namespaces | Dank Linux
documents.description: Layer namespaces DMS components
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide on configuring blur effects for Wayland layer shells, detailing the unique component namespaces used by DMS and offering specific configuration examples and performance tuning tips for different compositors like Hyprland and Niri.

documents.title: Setup | Dank Linux
documents.description: Deploy default DMS configuration files for your compositor
documents.summary: This document explains how the `dms setup` command generates default configuration files for compositors like niri or Hyprland, detailing that it only writes to non-existent or empty files.

documents.title: NixOS Installation | Dank Linux
documents.description: Install and configure DankMaterialShell on NixOS using native nixpkgs
documents.summary: This guide details how to install and configure DankMaterialShell on NixOS, covering requirements for the unstable channel, enabling core features like system monitoring and VPN, managing custom package versions via flakes, installing community plugins using a registry or manually, and deploying compositor-specific settings.

documents.title: NixOS Installation (Flake) | Dank Linux
documents.description: Install and configure DankMaterialShell on NixOS using flakes
documents.summary: This guide details multiple methods for installing DankMaterialShell on NixOS, covering system-wide via the NixOS module and per-user via home-manager. It also includes specific instructions for integrating with other tools like dgop and niri.

documents.title: Calendar Integration | Dank Linux
documents.description: Sync CalDAV calendars for dashboard integration
documents.summary: This document provides a guide on installing, configuring, and troubleshooting the synchronization of CalDAV-compatible calendars using vdirsyncer and khal. It details necessary commands for different distributions and sets up initial and automatic syncing.

documents.summary: This guide provides a comprehensive tutorial on building and shipping plugins for DankMaterialShell, detailing everything from setting up the development environment to creating fully functional widgets with settings and popout menus.

documents.title: Overview & Architecture | Dank Linux
documents.description: Modern Wayland desktop shell
documents.summary: This document introduces DankMaterialShell (dms), a Wayland desktop shell built with Quickshell and Go, detailing its extensive features for managing system tasks like applications, notifications, and hardware. It explains the architecture, component roles, and methods for integrating it into the desktop environment, including handling URLs and setting file associations.

documents.description: Install DankMaterialShell on your system
documents.summary: This guide provides detailed instructions on how to install DankMaterialShell across various Linux distributions, including specific commands and dependency requirements for Arch, Fedora/CentOS, Debian/Ubuntu, and OpenSUSE.

documents.description: <Hero

documents.title: Managing Your Installation | Dank Linux
documents.description: Update, configure, and manage DankMaterialShell
documents.summary: This guide provides comprehensive instructions on managing the DankMaterialShell (DMS) after installation, covering service management via systemd, manual launching methods, setting environment variables, updating packages, and proper uninstallation procedures.

documents.title: Brightness Control | Dank Linux
documents.description: Control backlight, LED, and DDC/I2C monitor brightness
documents.summary: This document explains the `dms brightness` command, a unified interface for managing screen brightness across various device types including backlights, LEDs, and external monitors via DDC/I2C. It provides detailed usage instructions for listing, getting, and setting brightness levels, including advanced topics like exponential scaling.

documents.title: Clipboard Manager | Dank Linux
documents.description: Wayland clipboard manager with history, search, and persistence
documents.summary: This document serves as a comprehensive guide and reference for the `dms cl` command-line utility, which functions as a Wayland clipboard manager offering features like history tracking, searching, and configuration management.

documents.title: Color Picker | Dank Linux
documents.description: Pick colors from anywhere on your Wayland screen with multiple output formats
documents.summary: This document explains how to use the `dms color pick` command, an interactive tool for Wayland that allows users to capture any pixel color from their screen and output it in various formats for different applications.

documents.title: Dank16 | Dank Linux
documents.description: Generate Base16 color palettes from a single color with intelligent contrast handling
documents.summary: This document explains the usage and features of the `dms dank16` command, a tool that generates complete, harmonious 16-color Base16 palettes from a single input color. It details various output formats (like Kitty or Alacritty configs), available contrast algorithms (Delta Phi Star and WCAG), and advanced options for customization.
